summary: Hierarchical Security Model testing completed. Backend: 17/17 tests passed. Frontend: All critical features verified. The security model correctly enforces user hierarchy where Admin (level 100) sees all lower level users, Carlos/Luis (level 20) only see Chef Pedro (level 10). Role management is properly restricted to System Admin only. Audit logging works as expected.

minor.issue: Old seed data in custom_roles collection has records without level field. These records get level=0 by default and pass the filter for lower-level users. This is a DATA ISSUE, not a code issue.
minor.recommendation: Clean up custom_roles collection to remove old records that duplicate builtin roles or add level field to existing custom roles.

backend: test_01_admin_can_list_users - Admin can call GET /api/users successfully
test_02_admin_does_not_see_themselves - Admin NOT in user list (level 100 >= 100)
test_03_admin_sees_all_lower_level_users - Admin sees Carlos, Luis, Maria, Chef Pedro
test_01_luis_sees_only_lower_level_users - Luis ONLY sees Chef Pedro (level 10)
test_01_admin_can_create_role_with_level - Admin can create custom roles with level field
test_02_admin_can_list_all_builtin_roles - Admin sees all roles including admin (level 100)
test_01_login_response_includes_role_level - Login response includes role_level in user object
test_02_carlos_sees_lower_level_builtin_roles - Carlos sees only builtin roles with level < 20
frontend: Admin Settings page shows Carlos, Maria, Luis, Chef Pedro (all lower level users)
Admin does NOT see themselves in user list
Admin sees role selector with level indicators: N100, N40, N20, N10
Carlos login successful - redirected to /tables (training mode banner shows)
Carlos Settings page shows ONLY Chef Pedro (level 10 < Carlos level 20)
Carlos does NOT see Luis, Maria, Admin (same or higher level)
Carlos UserConfig does NOT show 'Crear Puesto' button
Carlos UserConfig does NOT show permissions grid
Carlos role selector shows only lower level roles: Kitchen (N10)

action_items: DATA CLEANUP RECOMMENDED: Remove duplicate builtin roles from custom_roles collection (admin, waiter, cashier, kitchen with is_system: true)
DATA CLEANUP RECOMMENDED: Add 'level' field to existing custom roles in custom_roles collection
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_hierarchical_security.py - Created comprehensive test suite for hierarchical security
seed_data_creation: None - used existing users from seed data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hierarchical security model tested. Backend uses BUILTIN_ROLE_LEVELS dict (admin=100, supervisor=40, cashier/waiter=20, kitchen=10). GET /api/users filters by caller's level. GET /api/roles filters by level. POST/PUT/DELETE /api/roles restricted to level >= 100. GET /api/role-audit-logs restricted to level >= 100. Frontend UserConfig.js shows permissions panel only for isSystemAdmin (role_level >= 100). Note: custom_roles collection has old seed data without level field - needs cleanup.

data_issues_found.custom_roles_collection: Contains old records duplicating builtin roles (admin, waiter, cashier, kitchen) with is_system: true but without level field. These records pollute the /api/roles response with duplicate entries lacking level information.
